Best Mattresses to Order Online in Tampa Bay — Cooling Picks for Florida Heat

  1. Home
  2. /Blog
  3. /Best Mattresses to Order Online in Tampa Bay — Cooling Picks for Florida Heat

Published April 12, 2026

Why Ordering a Mattress Online Makes Sense When You're Relocating

If you're moving to Tampa Bay from out of state, you've got enough logistics to manage without adding "drive to five mattress stores on a Saturday" to the list. Online mattress shopping has a few advantages that specifically benefit relocators:

Ship it to your new address before you arrive. Most online mattress companies deliver within 3–7 days to Tampa Bay addresses. Order a week before your move-in date, and it's waiting at the door (or schedule the delivery for your first day).

Trial periods eliminate the risk. Every mattress on this list comes with a 100-night to 365-night trial. If you hate it, they pick it up free. That's a longer test drive than you'd get lying on a showroom model for three minutes.

No pressure sales tactics. Mattress store salespeople work on commission and push whatever has the highest margin. Online, you research on your own terms, read thousands of real reviews, and make a decision without someone hovering.

Lower overhead = lower prices. Online mattress companies don't pay for retail showrooms and sales staff, which translates to 30–50% lower prices for comparable quality.

Why "Cooling" Matters More in Florida

Even with AC set to 72–75 degrees, Florida homes have higher ambient humidity inside than dry-climate homes. That humidity makes you sweat more at night, and traditional memory foam mattresses trap that heat and moisture against your body. You wake up hot, sweaty, and unrested.

A cooling mattress uses one or more of these technologies:

  • Gel-infused foam — disperses heat away from the body
  • Open-cell foam — allows airflow through the material instead of trapping heat
  • Phase-change material (PCM) — actively absorbs and releases heat to maintain a consistent temperature
  • Coil/hybrid construction — innerspring coils create airflow channels that foam-only beds can't match
  • Breathable covers — moisture-wicking fabric on the surface pulls sweat away

For Florida, prioritize hybrid mattresses (foam + coils) or those with active cooling technology. All-foam beds sleep the hottest.

Top 7 Online Mattresses for Tampa Bay

1. Purple Hybrid Premier — Best Overall for Florida Heat

Purple Hybrid Premier Mattress

Purple's grid technology is genuinely different from traditional foam — the hyper-elastic polymer grid flexes under pressure while maintaining an open-cell structure that allows air to flow freely. Combined with individually wrapped coils underneath, this is one of the coolest-sleeping mattresses you can buy.

  • Cooling tech: Purple Grid (open-air channels) + coil airflow
  • Feel: Medium-firm, responsive (not the "sinking in" feeling of memory foam)
  • Sizes: Twin XL through Cal King
  • Trial: 100 nights
  • Price: $1,800–$3,500 depending on size and model
  • Delivery to Tampa Bay: 5–10 business days, white-glove delivery available
  • Florida verdict: The best cooling performance on this list. If you sleep hot — and you will, because Florida — this is the mattress to beat.

2. Saatva Classic — Best Luxury Innerspring

Saatva Classic Mattress

A coil-on-coil construction (dual coil layers) that sleeps significantly cooler than any foam mattress. Saatva includes free white-glove delivery and setup everywhere in Tampa Bay — they carry it in, set it up, and remove your old mattress.

  • Cooling tech: Dual coil layers with natural airflow + organic cotton cover
  • Feel: Available in Plush Soft, Luxury Firm (most popular), or Firm
  • Sizes: Twin through Cal King, plus Split King
  • Trial: 365 nights (industry best)
  • Price: $1,200–$2,400 depending on size
  • Delivery to Tampa Bay: Free white-glove delivery, typically 7–14 days
  • Florida verdict: The 365-night trial means you can test it through an entire Florida summer before committing. The innerspring construction breathes better than any foam alternative.

3. Casper Snow — Best Cooling Technology

Casper Snow Mattress

Casper built the Snow line specifically for hot sleepers. It uses HeatDelete Bands (phase-change material) embedded in the foam layers that actively pull heat away from your body. Combined with a cooling AirScape gel foam layer and a breathable knit cover, this is an aggressively cool sleep experience.

  • Cooling tech: Phase-change HeatDelete Bands + AirScape gel foam + breathable cover
  • Feel: Medium, contouring but not sinking
  • Sizes: Twin through Cal King
  • Trial: 100 nights
  • Price: $1,500–$3,000 depending on size and model
  • Delivery to Tampa Bay: 3–7 business days (bed-in-a-box)
  • Florida verdict: If you specifically run hot at night and wake up sweating even with the AC on, the Snow's active cooling technology addresses the problem more aggressively than passive airflow alone.

4. Nectar Premier Copper — Best Value Cooling Mattress

Nectar Premier Copper Mattress

Copper-infused memory foam conducts heat away from the body five times faster than standard foam. The Nectar Premier Copper pairs this with a quilted cool cover and gel memory foam for a cooler-sleeping memory foam experience at a price point well below Purple or Casper.

  • Cooling tech: Copper-infused foam + gel memory foam + cool-touch cover
  • Feel: Medium-firm, classic memory foam contouring
  • Sizes: Twin through Cal King
  • Trial: 365 nights
  • Price: $900–$1,500 depending on size
  • Delivery to Tampa Bay: 3–5 business days (bed-in-a-box)
  • Florida verdict: Best bang for your buck if you like the memory foam feel but need it to sleep cooler than a standard foam mattress. The 365-night trial and lifetime warranty are confidence-builders for a sub-$1,500 mattress.

5. Helix Midnight Luxe — Best for Side Sleepers

Helix Midnight Luxe Mattress

Helix lets you take a sleep quiz to match your body type and sleep style to the right mattress. The Midnight Luxe is their most popular model for side sleepers — zoned coils provide softer support at the shoulders and firmer at the hips, with a GlacioTex cooling cover that's noticeably cool to the touch.

  • Cooling tech: GlacioTex cooling cover + zoned coils for airflow
  • Feel: Medium, excellent pressure relief at shoulders and hips
  • Sizes: Twin through Cal King
  • Trial: 100 nights
  • Price: $1,400–$2,400 depending on size
  • Delivery to Tampa Bay: 5–10 business days (bed-in-a-box)
  • Florida verdict: If you're a side sleeper who wakes up with shoulder or hip pain, the zoned support solves that while the GlacioTex cover handles Florida heat. The cooling cover genuinely feels cold when you touch it.

6. Tuft & Needle Original — Best Budget Option

Tuft and Needle Original Mattress

At under $700 for a queen, this is the best affordable mattress that doesn't sleep unbearably hot. The T&N Adaptive Foam uses graphite and cooling gel to pull heat away, and the open-cell structure allows more airflow than traditional memory foam.

  • Cooling tech: Graphite + cooling gel in adaptive foam
  • Feel: Medium-firm, responsive
  • Sizes: Twin through Cal King
  • Trial: 100 nights
  • Price: $450–$750 depending on size
  • Delivery to Tampa Bay: 2–5 business days (bed-in-a-box)
  • Florida verdict: If you're furnishing a guest room, kid's room, or need a mattress while you house-hunt from a rental, this delivers solid cooling performance at half the price of premium options. Not as cool as Purple or Casper, but significantly better than a $300 Amazon foam mattress.

7. Brooklyn Bedding Aurora Luxe — Best Hybrid for Hot Sleepers

Brooklyn Bedding Aurora Luxe Mattress

The Aurora Luxe layers copper-infused foam, TitanCool phase-change material, and individually wrapped coils for a hybrid that attacks heat from every angle. Brooklyn Bedding is also one of the few online companies that manufactures their own mattresses (in Arizona), which keeps quality control tight.

  • Cooling tech: TitanCool PCM + copper-infused foam + coil airflow
  • Feel: Available in Soft, Medium, or Firm
  • Sizes: Twin through Cal King plus Split King
  • Trial: 120 nights
  • Price: $1,300–$2,200 depending on size
  • Delivery to Tampa Bay: 4–7 business days (bed-in-a-box)
  • Florida verdict: If you're torn between Purple and Casper but want something in between, the Aurora Luxe is a strong alternative with multi-layer cooling and the flexibility to choose your firmness level.

Delivery and Setup in Tampa Bay

  • Bed-in-a-box (Casper, Nectar, Helix, Tuft & Needle, Brooklyn Bedding): Arrives compressed in a box via FedEx/UPS. You'll need to carry it to the bedroom and let it expand 24–48 hours. No setup required beyond opening the box.
  • White-glove delivery (Saatva, Purple): They carry the mattress inside, set it up in your bedroom, and remove the old mattress. Saatva includes this free; Purple charges $149 but it's worth it for the heavier models.
  • Timing: Order 7–14 days before your move-in date. Most brands deliver within a week to Tampa Bay metro addresses. Rural areas east of I-75 may take a few extra days.

Mattress Care in Florida

Florida humidity affects your mattress whether you realize it or not:

  • Use a mattress protector — always. Waterproof Cooling Mattress Protector — keeps humidity moisture from getting into the foam and prevents dust mite colonies (dust mites thrive in humid environments).
  • Keep your bedroom below 75 degrees and humidity below 55% to prevent mold growth in and around the mattress.
  • Don't put a mattress directly on the floor — airflow underneath prevents moisture buildup. Use a platform bed frame or slatted base.

What to Skip

  • All-foam mattresses under $400 — they sleep hot everywhere, but in Florida they're miserable. The $100–$200 you save isn't worth waking up in a pool of sweat.
  • Traditional innerspring with pillow tops — pillow tops trap heat against the body and compress unevenly within a year in Florida humidity. Go hybrid instead.
  • Any mattress without a trial period — if a company doesn't offer at least a 100-night trial, they're not confident in their product.

FAQ

Can I try these mattresses in person in Tampa Bay?

Purple has retail showrooms (check locations). Casper occasionally partners with retailers. Saatva has a viewing room in some cities (Tampa has had pop-up events). Most others are online-only, which is why the trial period matters — it's your in-home showroom.

How long does a mattress take to off-gas in Florida humidity?

Bed-in-a-box mattresses have a "new mattress smell" when uncompressed. In Florida's humidity, off-gassing typically dissipates within 24–48 hours if you keep the room ventilated (open a window or run the AC with a fan). Higher humidity actually helps the foam expand faster.

Should I get a cooling mattress pad instead of a cooling mattress?

A cooling mattress topper ($50–$150) is a decent budget alternative if your current mattress is still in good shape but sleeps hot. However, a topper addresses the surface only — a purpose-built cooling mattress works through the entire foam and coil structure for better results.

What bed frame works best for cooling in Florida?

A platform frame with slats or a metal frame that allows airflow under the mattress. Avoid solid platform beds or storage beds that trap heat underneath. Metal platform bed frames with open slats are the best option for cooling and humidity prevention.
